title: Ifloga thellungiana
intro: Ifloga thellungiana, also known as the Himalayan Ifloga or Thellungi's Ifloga, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ifloga thellungiana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Ifloga thellungiana is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ifloga thellungiana is classified within the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. This classification places it among the flowering plants, which are further divided into various families and orders based on shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Ifloga thellungiana specifically belongs to the genus Ifloga, which comprises several species of plants native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The family Asteraceae, to which Ifloga thellungiana belongs, is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, encompassing a wide range of species. By categorizing Ifloga thellungiana within the Asteraceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
